title: Acnistus glabratus
intro: Acnistus glabratus, commonly known as the night-blooming jasmine or night-scented jasmine, is a flowering plant native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Acnistus glabratus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is typically used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and relieve symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Acnistus glabratus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Brazil and other surrounding countries. The plant is placed in the genus Acnistus, which comprises a small number of species. Acnistus glabratus is a shrub or small tree, characterized by its simple, alternate leaves and small, white, tubular flowers. The exact classification within the Solanaceae family is in the tribe Hyoscyameae.
